_doc: Operational auth contract for OAuth-backed remote MCP servers. Server definitions live in mcp.json; this file says how to authenticate them on a host without copying opaque token stores between machines.

policy.host_model: authenticate once per host through mcp-remote; stdio-compatible clients on that host reuse ~/.mcp-auth
policy.headless_model: remote OAuth servers are optional in unattended strict configs unless the host already has a valid ~/.mcp-auth cache
policy.preferred_long_term: use official hosted MCPs through mcp-remote first; graduate to a narrow local facade only if hosted MCP behavior is insufficient

linear.account_boundary: acts with the connected user's Linear workspace permissions

claude.setup: Run `mcp-auth login linear` once on the target host, then use the synced Linear MCP entry normally.

opencode.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; auth state comes from ~/.mcp-auth after `mcp-auth login linear`.

codex.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; Codex does not need native remote OAuth support for this server.

linear.vm_setup: Run `mcp-auth vm-login linear <vm-host>` from the laptop. It opens an SSH local-forward for the OAuth callback and runs the VM-side login command.
linear.rollback: Use the client's disconnect/logout flow; remove the server with mcp-sync only if the connector should disappear from all clients.

sentry.account_boundary: acts with the connected user's Sentry organization and project permissions

claude.setup: Run `mcp-auth login sentry` once on the target host, then use the synced Sentry MCP entry normally.

opencode.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; auth state comes from ~/.mcp-auth after `mcp-auth login sentry`.

codex.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; Codex does not need native remote OAuth support for this server.

sentry.vm_setup: Run `mcp-auth vm-login sentry <vm-host>` from the laptop. It opens an SSH local-forward for the OAuth callback and runs the VM-side login command.
sentry.rollback: Use the client's disconnect/logout flow; remove the server with mcp-sync only if the connector should disappear from all clients.

notion.account_boundary: acts with the connected user's Notion permissions

claude.setup: Run `mcp-auth login notion` once on the target host, then use the synced Notion MCP entry normally.

opencode.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; auth state comes from ~/.mcp-auth after `mcp-auth login notion`.

codex.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; Codex does not need native remote OAuth support for this server.

notion.vm_setup: Run `mcp-auth vm-login notion <vm-host>` from the laptop. It opens an SSH local-forward for the OAuth callback and runs the VM-side login command.
notion.rollback: Use the client's disconnect/logout flow; remove the server with mcp-sync only if the connector should disappear from all clients.

granola.account_boundary: acts with the connected Granola account and active workspace

claude.setup: Run `mcp-auth login granola` once on the target host, then use the synced Granola MCP entry normally.

opencode.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; auth state comes from ~/.mcp-auth after `mcp-auth login granola`.

codex.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; Codex does not need native remote OAuth support for this server.

granola.vm_setup: Run `mcp-auth vm-login granola <vm-host>` from the laptop. It opens an SSH local-forward for the OAuth callback and runs the VM-side login command.
granola.rollback: Use the client's disconnect/logout flow; remove the server with mcp-sync only if the connector should disappear from all clients.

cloudflare.account_boundary: acts with the connected user's Cloudflare account permissions or the configured Cloudflare API token scopes

claude.setup: Run `mcp-auth login cloudflare` once on the target host, then use the synced Cloudflare MCP entry normally.

opencode.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; auth state comes from ~/.mcp-auth after `mcp-auth login cloudflare`.

codex.setup: Use the synced stdio MCP entry; Codex does not need native remote OAuth support for this server.

cloudflare.vm_setup: Run `mcp-auth vm-login cloudflare <vm-host>` from the laptop. It opens an SSH local-forward for the OAuth callback and runs the VM-side login command.
cloudflare.rollback: Use the client's disconnect/logout flow; remove the server with mcp-sync only if the connector should disappear from all clients.

slack.account_boundary: acts with the connected Slack user's workspace permissions and the Slack app scopes configured in SLACK_MCP_SCOPES
slack.host_requirements: Set SLACK_MCP_CLIENT_ID and SLACK_MCP_CLIENT_SECRET, or SLACK_MCP_CLIENT_INFO_FILE, on each host. The Slack app must include http://127.0.0.1:3339/oauth/callback as an OAuth redirect URL unless SLACK_MCP_CALLBACK_PORT is overridden.

claude.setup: Configure the host-local Slack MCP app credentials, run `mcp-auth login slack` once on the target host, then use the synced Slack MCP entry normally.

opencode.setup: Use the synced wrapper MCP entry; auth state comes from ~/.mcp-auth after `mcp-auth login slack`.

codex.setup: Use the synced wrapper MCP entry; Codex does not need native remote OAuth support for this server.

slack.vm_setup: Install host-local Slack MCP app credentials on the VM, then run `mcp-auth vm-login slack <vm-host>` from the laptop. It opens an SSH local-forward for the OAuth callback and runs the VM-side login command.
slack.rollback: Use the client's disconnect/logout flow; remove the server with mcp-sync only if the connector should disappear from all clients.
